project.name: VAF Agentic Architect v2
project.owner: ZenCloud Global Consultants / Myint Enterprises Pty Ltd
project.description: Claude-powered agentic Enterprise Architect that autonomously generates EA artefacts (governance, strategy, design) using Velocity Architecture Framework rules and constraints

components.name: Agentic Architect
components.responsibility: Reads VAF knowledge base, applies framework rules, orchestrates task execution
components.bestPractice: Single responsibility: reasoning only, no side effects

components.name: Orchestrator
components.responsibility: Routes tasks, manages workflow state, sequences agent calls
components.bestPractice: Keep orchestration logic flat and readable, delegate complexity to agents

components.name: Action Engine
components.responsibility: Executes deterministic operations (GitHub commits, API calls) without LLM overhead
inputs: Action directive (generate artefact, commit file, etc.)
components.bestPractice: Use pure functions, not tool calls, for infrastructure operations

layers.name: Knowledge Layer
layers.description: VAF framework, governance rules, and artefact templates

components.name: VAF Knowledge Base
components.responsibility: Governs all agent reasoning; authoritative source of VAF rules

content.governance_patterns: Decision authority mappings, governance drag analysis, altitude collapse detection
content.decision_trees: Flow-based frameworks for artefact generation decisions
content.best_practices: Curated VAF-specific approaches to enterprise architecture
content.constraints: Compliance rules, naming conventions, template requirements

components.loadingStrategy: Runtime injection via prompt context; never hardcoded
components.versioning: Semantic versioning; agent tracks KB version used for each artefact

components.name: Framework Rules Engine
components.responsibility: Enforces VAF constraints during and after artefact generation
rules: Artefact structure validation (required sections, tone consistency)
Governance drag detection (implicit vs. explicit decision points)
Cross-artefact consistency (strategy aligns with governance, design aligns with both)
components.bestPractice: Separate validation agent (single responsibility) from generation agent

components.name: Artefact Templates
components.responsibility: Blueprint for governance docs, strategy maps, design specs
components.bestPractice: Templates externalized for easy iteration by non-technical stakeholders

components.name: GitHub API
components.responsibility: Commit generated artefacts, read templates, manage branches

operations.constraint: Audit trail required (timestamp, agent action, user attribution)

components.rateLimit: Handled via exponential backoff in action engine
components.bestPractice: Direct calls eliminate MCP abstraction overhead and ambiguity

components.name: Claude API

parameters.context: VAF knowledge base + user request + prior artefacts (if consolidating)

components.costTracking: Log tokens per artefact type for billing/optimization
components.bestPractice: Separate API key injection for Phase 1 (your account) and Phase 2 (client account)

components.name: Governance Artefacts
components.responsibility: Authoritative decision governance, compliance mappings, drag analysis

generation.constraints: Must validate against VAF governance rules

components.name: Strategy Documents
components.responsibility: Multi-year capability roadmaps, initiative sequences, dependency maps

generation.constraints: Must align with governance artefact decisions

components.name: Design Specifications
components.responsibility: Service meshes, capability models, integration patterns, implementation constraints

generation.constraints: Must validate against governance AND strategy artefacts

components.name: Docker Container
components.responsibility: Sealed runtime with no external egress except Claude API + GitHub
components.networkPolicy: Egress only to api.anthropic.com and api.github.com; no other outbound traffic

components.name: Kubernetes Orchestration
components.responsibility: Auto-scaling, health checks, namespace isolation, secret management

manifests.configMap: k8s/configmap.yaml (non-secret config: KB version, template paths)
manifests.secret: k8s/secret.yaml (API keys, GitHub tokens per tenant)

components.namespaceStrategy: Phase 1: vaf-production (your tenant) | Phase 2: vaf-client-{id} (per client)
components.bestPractice: Keep manifests clean; drive differences via namespace-scoped secrets

components.name: Cloud Runtime
options: AWS App Runner (recommended for Phase 1, AWS expertise)
components.responsibility: Serverless execution, no infrastructure management, auto-scaling, pay-per-execution
components.phase1Recommendation: AWS App Runner (aligns with your deep AWS knowledge)
components.deploymentMechanism: GitHub Actions → Container Registry → Cloud Runtime

steps: Deploy to Cloud Runtime (gcloud / aws cli)

components.bestPractice: Cloud-agnostic so Phase 2 clients can deploy to their own accounts

layers.name: v1 Baseline (Operational Foundation)
layers.description: Existing infrastructure from Version 1; continues to feed the agent

components.name: Studio
components.responsibility: Knowledge authoring, VAF pattern curation, template maintenance
components.outputs: Updated knowledge base (committed to repo, agent reads at runtime)

components.name: Portal
components.responsibility: Display generated artefacts, manage access, request tracking
components.inputs: Generated EA artefacts (from agent) + user requests
components.outputs: Curated view for executives, architects, compliance teams

components.name: Research
components.responsibility: Audit reports, regulatory data, benchmarks for agent context
components.inputs: Evidence from Queensland Audit Office, Deloitte, NSW Auditor-General, VAGO
components.outputs: Structured data feeding into governance and strategy artefacts

components.name: Framework
components.responsibility: Velocity Architecture Framework rules, concepts, patterns
components.inputs: Practitioner feedback, field observations, case studies
components.outputs: Evolved knowledge base (fed to v2 agent)

dataFlow.action: Routes to Agentic Architect with knowledge base and request
Reasons over VAF rules, generates task plan (which artefacts needed, validation rules)
Executes task plan sequentially; spawns generation agents
Each generates its artefact type, produces markdown output
Checks each artefact against VAF rules, flags deviations
If multiple artefacts, ensures cross-alignment (governance ← strategy ← design)
Commits all artefacts to GitHub (direct API calls, no tool ambiguity)

phases.name: Your Tenant (Production)
phases.target: Your cloud account (AWS recommended, aligned with your expertise)
phases.compute: AWS App Runner or Google Cloud Run

storage.artefacts: Your cloud blob storage (S3 or GCS)

credentials.claudeApiKey: Your account, injected as env var

phases.isolation: Namespace-scoped; all requests tagged with your tenant ID

phases.name: Client Tenant (Future)
phases.target: Client's cloud account (their choice: AWS, Azure, GCP)
phases.compute: Same Docker image, client's App Runner/Cloud Run

credentials.claudeApiKey: Client's account, injected as env var

phases.isolation: Complete data and credential separation via env vars and Kubernetes namespaces
phases.mechanism: Same Dockerfile, different ConfigMap/Secret per client

phases.name: Diagnostic Mode (Local)
phases.target: Local developer machine or CI environment
phases.compute: Docker + Kubernetes minikube or Docker Compose

architecture.coreDeploymentDiagram: Container (Docker) → Registry (GHCR) → Cloud Runtime (App Runner/Cloud Run) → Kubernetes Namespace (tenant-scoped) → Storage (S3/GCS) + GitHub
architecture.multiTenancyMechanism: Environment variables + Kubernetes namespaces. Same Docker image, different secrets per tenant.
architecture.networkBoundary: Ingress: User requests via HTTPS. Egress: API calls only to api.anthropic.com (Claude) and api.github.com (GitHub). No other outbound traffic.

bestPractices.name: Tool Calls Over MCP
bestPractices.principle: Use direct API calls (tool calls) instead of MCP abstraction for deterministic operations
bestPractices.application: GitHub operations: commit, read, createPullRequest — all direct functions in Action Engine
bestPractices.rationale: MCP adds abstraction overhead and agent cognitive load. Direct calls are deterministic, auditable, faster.
bestPractices.implementation: Pure functions in action-engine.ts; no LLM-based tool selection
bestPractices.costBenefit: Eliminates ~20% of token overhead from tool-call parsing

bestPractices.name: Direct Function Calls Over Tool Calls
bestPractices.principle: For operations not requiring language reasoning, use pure functions in orchestration layer
bestPractices.application: File commits, timestamps, API posts, database writes — bypass agent entirely
bestPractices.rationale: Infrastructure operations are deterministic; no need for LLM reasoning. Reduces token usage, increases speed.
bestPractices.implementation: Separate orchestration.ts module; pure functions called directly from Orchestrator
bestPractices.costBenefit: 50% faster execution for commits; 30% token reduction

bestPractices.name: Single Tool Per Agent
bestPractices.principle: Each agent gets one and only one tool/responsibility
bestPractices.application: Governance Agent (1 tool: generate governance doc); Strategy Agent (1 tool: generate strategy); Design Agent (1 tool: generate design spec)
bestPractices.rationale: Reduces agent cognitive load, eliminates tool-selection ambiguity, improves reliability
bestPractices.implementation: Split monolithic 'artefact generator' into three specialist agents

bestPractices.name: Single-Responsibility Agents
bestPractices.principle: Each agent does one conceptual job well; no mixing of generation, validation, and side effects
bestPractices.application: Generation Agent (writes markdown). Validation Agent (checks constraints). Publisher Agent (commits). NOT all in one.
bestPractices.rationale: Cleaner prompts, easier testing, reduced non-determinism
bestPractices.implementation: Separate prompt files for generation vs. validation; different agents invoked sequentially
bestPractices.testingBenefit: Unit test each agent's output contract independently

bestPractices.name: Externalize Prompts
bestPractices.principle: Store prompts outside code; load at runtime from repository
bestPractices.application: All VAF governance rules, generation instructions, validation rules in GitHub repo (/prompts/ directory)
bestPractices.rationale: Non-technical stakeholders (policy teams) can iterate without code changes. Version control, rollback, A/B testing.
bestPractices.implementation: Load prompts at runtime from GitHub; cache locally for performance
bestPractices.governance: Pull request workflow for prompt changes; approval required before production use

bestPractices.name: Responsible AI Consortium
bestPractices.principle: Generate with multiple models (Claude + GPT-4) in parallel; consolidate via reasoning agent
bestPractices.application: Invoke Claude Sonnet AND OpenAI GPT-4 simultaneously for each artefact. Reasoning agent (Claude) synthesizes.
bestPractices.rationale: Higher accuracy via cross-model agreement. Reduced bias from single-model perspective. Robustness to model updates.
bestPractices.implementation: Parallel agent spawning; prompt both models identically; reasoning consolidation step
bestPractices.trade-off: 2x API costs for generation; offset by higher output quality and reduced human revision

bestPractices.name: Separate Workflow from MCP Server
bestPractices.principle: Workflow engine runs as REST API; MCP server is thin adapter layer only
bestPractices.application: VAF agent logic in app.ts (REST endpoints). MCP server in mcp-server.ts (forwards calls to REST API).
bestPractices.rationale: Independent scaling, long-term adaptability. MCP changes don't affect core workflow.
bestPractices.implementation: Express.js REST API + separate Anthropic MCP SDK wrapper
bestPractices.scalability: Can scale API replicas independently from MCP clients

bestPractices.name: Containerized Deployment
bestPractices.principle: Package everything in Docker; orchestrate with Kubernetes; deploy to managed cloud runtime
bestPractices.application: Dockerfile (sealed runtime). Kubernetes manifests (deployment, service, configmap, secret). GitHub Actions CI/CD.
bestPractices.rationale: Portability across clouds. Reproducibility. Automatic scaling. Security (isolation, RBAC). Observability integration.
bestPractices.implementation: Docker image → GHCR → Cloud Run / App Runner
bestPractices.phasedDeployment: Phase 1 (your tenant, your cloud). Phase 2 (client tenant, client cloud). Same image, different env vars.

bestPractices.name: KISS Principle
bestPractices.principle: Keep orchestration logic flat, readable, and simple. Delegate complexity to agents.
bestPractices.application: Orchestrator.ts is <200 lines. Task routing is straightforward. No deep inheritance, no elaborate patterns.
bestPractices.rationale: Simplicity == reliability. Easier for AI-assisted development (Claude Code). Extensible as new patterns emerge.
bestPractices.implementation: Function-driven design. Minimal abstraction layers. Clear data flow (no hidden state).
bestPractices.ai-friendliness: Claude Code can understand and refactor simple code; complex systems resist AI augmentation

llmIntegration.costTracking: Logged per request for billing and optimization

integration.protocol: MCP (Model Context Protocol) for external client access

pipeline: Deploy: gcloud run deploy OR aws apprunner (depending on Phase)
Test: Smoke tests (health endpoint, artefact generation sample)

observability.tracing: Distributed tracing (OpenTelemetry) for agent decision flow
focus: Agent decisions (which artefacts generated, validation results)
Cost tracking (Claude API tokens per artefact)

network.egress: Only to api.anthropic.com (Claude) and api.github.com (GitHub). No other outbound traffic.
network.ingress: HTTPS only. User requests only; no server-to-server webhooks.
network.philosophy: Lockdown by default. Minimal attack surface.

data.isolation: Per-tenant via environment variables and Kubernetes namespaces
data.storage: Client data never mixed. Separate S3 buckets or GCS projects per Phase 2 client.
data.encryption: At rest (cloud provider managed); in transit (TLS 1.3)

auditability.loggingRequirement: Every artefact commit logged with: timestamp, agent action, user attribution, VAF rules applied, tokens consumed
auditability.rollbackCapability: All commits are reversible; prompt versions pinned per artefact

determinism.requirement: Infrastructure operations must be deterministic (direct functions, not tool calls)
determinism.testing: Repeat same request → same artefact (modulo minor LLM variation; content deterministic)
determinism.constraint: No random decisions in generation pipeline; all variation must be explicit (user input or multi-model consensus)

keyFiles.responsibility: HTTP server, endpoint definitions, request routing
Business logic: task planning, agent sequencing, error recovery
Individual agent implementations; each does one thing
Agent instructions; editable by non-technical stakeholders

nextSteps.task: Set up TypeScript project with Express.js and Anthropic SDK
checklist: Create app.ts with basic HTTP server
Implement state transitions (planning → generation → validation → publishing)
Load prompts from GitHub at runtime
Call Claude API, parse markdown output
Add retry logic and rate limiting
Load VAF constraint rules from KB
Add Docker build and push step
Forward tool calls to REST API
Test with Claude Desktop or LM Studio

nextSteps.task: Deploy Phase 1 to Cloud Run (AWS or GCP)
checklist: Configure GitHub Actions secrets (API keys, cloud credentials)

phase1: Agent successfully generates governance, strategy, design artefacts
All artefacts committed to GitHub with audit trail
Container runs in Cloud Run without errors
Health check endpoint responds within 1 second
phase2Readiness: Environment variables parameterize all tenant-specific config
Documentation explains how clients deploy to their own cloud accounts
Test deployment to second cloud provider (to verify cloud-agnosticism)
productionReadiness: All agent decisions logged and auditable
Error recovery tested (agent failure → graceful degradation)
Security audit passed (network egress, data isolation, auth)

references.paper: A Practical Guide for Designing, Developing, and Deploying Production-Grade Agentic AI Workflows (Bandara et al., arXiv:2512.08769)

keyLearnings.determinism: Direct function calls and single-responsibility agents improve determinism 40%+
keyLearnings.costs: Parallel multi-model generation costs 2x but improves output quality significantly
keyLearnings.scalability: Kubernetes + Cloud Run scales automatically; no infrastructure management

metadata.purpose: Self-explanatory codebase specification; should answer 95% of questions about VAF v2 architecture, design decisions, and deployment without needing to read code
metadata.updateFrequency: Regenerate when architecture changes; keep in sync with codebase
